dynasty.description: hellenistic successor state, three centuries of greek-macedonian rule over egypt
hellenistic successor state, persian to mesopotamian span, gradual territorial dissolution
hellenistic successor state, macedonian heartland, roman conquest terminus
hellenistic successor state, anatolian greek cultural center, roman bequest conclusion
first imperial dynasty, unification of china under centralized legalist state
foundational imperial era, consolidation of bureaucracy, confucian state ideology
cosmopolitan golden age, major cultural, military and trade expansion
economic and technological expansion, bureaucratic refinement, military pressure from north
mongol-led empire governing china, integration into wider eurasian system
restoration of han rule, maritime expansion, strong centralized imperial administration
manchu-led dynasty, territorial peak of imperial china, gradual internal and external pressure
first great persian empire, imperial administration across anatolia, mesopotamia, and iranian plateau, ended by macedonian conquest
iranian imperial dynasty balancing hellenistic and eastern traditions, decentralized feudal structure, long rivalry with rome
last pre-islamic persian empire, highly centralized state, revival of iranian identity, constant conflict with eastern rome
early republican phase, expansion across italy, development of senate-led political system and military citizen state
imperial phase following augustus, centralized autocracy with extensive territorial control across europe, north africa, and near east
period of administrative fragmentation, christianization of state, increasing pressure on western provinces leading to eventual collapse in the west
eastern continuation of roman state, centered on constantinople, preservation of roman law and administration under greek cultural influence
pan-indian empire, ashoka's dharma, buddhist propagation
classical golden age, sanskrit renaissance, temple university foundations
maritime power, south indian hegemony, southeast asian cultural diffusion
confederacy against mughal fragmentation, pre-colonial military mobilization
